industry.name: Additive Manufacturing
industry.description: 3D printing, metal fabrication, and advanced materials — one of the fastest-growing sectors in U.S. defense, aerospace, and medical manufacturing. 84 roles across 5 clusters.

roles.title: AM Design Engineer 1
roles.description: Creates and prepares digital 3D models optimized for additive manufacturing, translating product concepts into printable build files.

roles.title: DfAM Engineer
roles.description: Specializes in redesigning parts to exploit additive-specific freedoms — lattices, consolidation, topology optimization — for weight and performance gains.

roles.title: AM Applications Engineer
roles.description: Bridges customers and engineering — evaluates AM use cases, selects processes, and proves out applications from concept to qualified part.

roles.title: Senior AM Design Engineer
roles.description: Leads complex AM part design programs, sets design standards, and mentors junior designers across multiple printing technologies.

roles.title: AM Simulation Engineer
roles.description: Models the printing process to predict distortion, residual stress, and build failures before parts are ever printed.

roles.title: Principal AM Engineer
roles.description: Sets the technical direction for additive across an organization, drives R&D, and owns qualification and IP strategy.

roles.title: AM Materials Lab Technician
roles.description: Prepares and characterizes feedstock powders and printed samples, supporting materials engineers in the lab.

roles.title: AM Materials Engineer
roles.description: Develops and qualifies the metal and polymer feedstocks used in printing, studying how materials respond to lasers and electron beams.

roles.title: AM Process Development Engineer
roles.description: Dials in print parameters for new materials and geometries to achieve dense, defect-free, repeatable builds.

roles.title: Senior AM Materials Engineer
roles.description: Leads materials R&D and qualification across programs, owning the alloy roadmap and resolving complex materials failures.

roles.title: AM Research Scientist
roles.description: Conducts fundamental research into new additive processes, materials, and physics, often bridging academia and industry.

roles.title: 3D Printer Operator
roles.description: Sets up printers, loads feedstock, starts and monitors build jobs, and performs routine machine upkeep.

roles.title: AM Machine Technician
roles.description: Operates and maintains industrial additive machines, calibrating systems and troubleshooting build issues across shifts.

roles.title: Senior AM Technician
roles.description: Runs the most complex builds, leads troubleshooting, and trains newer operators on production floors.

roles.title: AM Manufacturing Engineer
roles.description: Owns the production workflow — build planning, machine scheduling, and throughput — turning prototypes into serial production.

roles.title: AM Production Supervisor
roles.description: Leads the print floor team, manages schedules and safety, and is accountable for daily production output.

roles.title: AM Operations Manager
roles.description: Runs the entire additive production operation — capacity, cost, quality, and delivery across the machine fleet.

roles.title: AM Post-Processing Technician
roles.description: Handles the final stages of printing — removing supports, cleaning, finishing, and preparing parts for use.

roles.title: AM Quality Technician
roles.description: Inspects printed parts against specifications using calibrated tools and documents quality results.

roles.title: NDT Technician (AM)
roles.description: Uses non-destructive testing — CT, X-ray, ultrasound — to find internal defects and porosity in printed parts.

roles.title: AM Quality Engineer
roles.description: Owns the quality system for additive parts — qualification, inspection plans, root-cause analysis, and standards compliance.

roles.title: Senior AM Quality Engineer
roles.description: Leads qualification of additive parts for regulated industries (aerospace, medical) and owns audit and compliance strategy.

roles.title: AM Sales Engineer
roles.description: Sells additive solutions by translating customer problems into technical proposals, demos, and qualified opportunities.

roles.title: AM Software Engineer
roles.description: Develops the software that powers additive — slicers, build-prep tools, toolpath generators, and machine control.

roles.title: AM Supply Chain Analyst
roles.description: Manages powder and feedstock inventory, sourcing, and demand planning to keep production supplied.

roles.title: Business Development Manager (AM)
roles.description: Drives revenue growth by opening new markets, building partnerships, and growing strategic accounts for additive offerings.

roles.title: Director of Additive Manufacturing
roles.description: Owns the additive manufacturing function end-to-end — strategy, P&L, technology, and team — at the executive level.

roles.title: AM CAD/Reverse Engineering Specialist
roles.description: Converts physical parts and scan data into clean printable CAD models, repairing meshes and reconstructing geometry.

roles.title: AM Automation Engineer
roles.description: Automates additive production lines — robotics, part handling, and the digital thread connecting machines.

roles.title: Powder Metallurgy Technician
roles.description: Manages metal powder feedstock — characterization, recycling, and quality — that feeds metal printers.

roles.title: AM Build Preparation Specialist
roles.description: Prepares build files — orienting parts, generating supports, and nesting for maximum yield per build.

roles.title: AM Maintenance Technician
roles.description: Keeps the additive machine fleet running — diagnosing and repairing lasers, optics, motion, and gas systems.

roles.title: CNC/Finishing Specialist (AM)
roles.description: Performs the machining and finishing operations that bring printed parts to final tolerance and surface quality.

roles.title: Metrology Engineer (AM)
roles.description: Owns dimensional measurement for additive parts — CMM, scanning, and the inspection plans that prove parts meet spec.

roles.title: AM Product Manager
roles.description: Owns the roadmap for an additive product or platform, balancing customer needs, technology, and business goals.

roles.title: AM Training & Applications Specialist
roles.description: Trains customers and internal teams on additive technology and develops new application examples and documentation.

roles.title: AM Drafter
roles.degree_detail: Associate degree in CAD/drafting or equivalent training
roles.description: Produces detailed 2D and 3D technical drawings for additive manufacturing parts, translating engineering specs into manufacturable build files.

roles.title: CAM Programmer (AM)
roles.degree_detail: Associate degree in manufacturing technology or CAM programming
roles.description: Generates machine instructions and toolpaths from CAD models for hybrid AM/CNC workflows, ensuring printable geometry and downstream finishing compatibility.

roles.title: Reverse Engineering Technician (AM)
roles.degree_detail: Associate degree or certificate in CAD/metrology
roles.description: Captures legacy parts via 3D scanning and reconstructs printable CAD geometry, enabling AM reproduction of out-of-production or replacement components.

roles.title: Generative Design Engineer (AM)
roles.description: Uses algorithm-driven design tools to produce optimized geometries that meet structural targets while exploiting additive freedoms like internal lattices and conformal cooling.

roles.title: Tooling & Fixture Engineer (AM)
roles.description: Designs 3D-printed and conventional jigs, fixtures, and tooling that support AM production and downstream assembly workflows.

roles.title: AM Solutions Architect
roles.description: Designs end-to-end AM production architectures spanning design intake, materials selection, process selection, and qualification — for enterprise customers and internal programs.

roles.title: Lead Generative Design Engineer
roles.description: Leads generative design programs, sets design-for-AM standards across the org, and mentors design engineers on optimization workflows.

roles.title: AM Standards & Compliance Lead
roles.description: Owns AM standards and compliance strategy — interfaces with ASTM F42 / ISO TC 261 and translates standards into internal qualification procedures.

roles.title: Lead Tooling Engineer (AM)
roles.description: Heads AM tooling and fixture development — drives standardization of jig/fixture design across product lines.

roles.title: Powder Handling Technician (AM)
roles.degree_detail: Associate degree or certificate in materials/manufacturing tech
roles.description: Handles, conditions, and recycles metal/polymer powders for AM machines — manages powder lots, contamination control, and inert-atmosphere handling.

roles.title: Materials Test Technician (AM)
roles.degree_detail: Associate degree in materials science or mechanical tech
roles.description: Runs mechanical and physical property tests on AM coupons and witness samples to verify build quality.

roles.title: Metallography Technician (AM)
roles.description: Prepares and images metallographic cross-sections of AM parts to evaluate porosity, grain structure, and defect distribution.

roles.title: AM Process Lab Assistant
roles.description: Supports process development lab work — sample prep, parameter sweeps, build logs, and machine babysitting during R&D runs.

roles.title: Extrusion Process Engineer (AM)
roles.description: Develops and qualifies extrusion-based AM processes — polymer feedstock, pellet extruders, and large-format FFF systems.

roles.title: AM Polymer Materials Engineer
roles.degree_detail: BS/MS Polymer Science or Materials Engineering
roles.description: Specializes in polymer feedstocks for SLA/DLP/FDM/SLS — qualifies new resins and powders against application requirements.

roles.title: Metallurgical Engineer (AM)
roles.description: Owns metallurgy for metal AM — alloy selection, post-build heat treatment design, and microstructure-property correlation.

roles.title: AM Process Validation Engineer
roles.description: Validates AM processes for regulated production — runs IQ/OQ/PQ protocols, DOE studies, and lock-down documentation.

roles.title: Lead Materials Development Engineer (AM)
roles.description: Leads materials development programs — owns the roadmap for new feedstocks and oversees the team qualifying them through to production.

roles.title: Principal Metallurgist (AM)
roles.description: Principal-level metallurgical authority for AM metal programs — final reviewer on alloy decisions, post-build thermal treatments, and failure investigations.

roles.title: AM R&D Manager
roles.description: Manages the AM R&D function — sets research priorities, owns IP strategy, and bridges advanced research and production engineering.

roles.title: Industrial Manufacturing Technician (AM)
roles.description: Generalist production technician on an AM shop floor — runs builds, follows routing, and supports preventive maintenance.

roles.title: AM Production Operator
roles.description: Front-line operator running production AM machines on shift — load material, start builds, monitor for faults, hand off to post-processing.

roles.title: AM Process Engineer
roles.description: Owns AM process parameters and build planning for a production cell — optimizes for yield, cycle time, and quality.

roles.title: AM Production Engineer
roles.description: Designs production cells and workflows for AM — capacity planning, cell layout, and KPI/OEE management.

roles.title: AM Plant Manager
roles.description: Runs an AM production plant or factory floor — full P&L responsibility, safety, headcount, and output.

roles.title: AM Production Director
roles.description: Senior executive over AM production across one or more sites — strategic operations, capex, and org design.

roles.title: Lead AM Process Engineer
roles.description: Leads the AM process engineering function — owns parameter libraries, qualification cadence, and process engineer mentorship.

roles.title: AM Facility Engineering Lead
roles.description: Owns AM facility infrastructure — inert gas, powder handling rooms, HVAC, fire suppression, and EH&S compliance.

roles.title: Principal Manufacturing Engineer (AM)
roles.description: Principal-level manufacturing engineer — final technical authority on AM production architectures and qualification packages.

roles.title: AM Heat Treatment Technician
roles.description: Runs heat-treatment, stress-relief, and HIP cycles on AM metal parts to achieve target mechanical properties.

roles.title: AM Surface Finishing Technician
roles.description: Removes supports and finishes AM part surfaces to spec — blasting, tumbling, polishing, and surface roughness verification.

roles.title: Quality Control Technician (AM)
roles.description: Runs incoming, in-process, and final inspections of AM parts against drawings and specs.

roles.title: AM Post-Processing Engineer
roles.description: Engineers post-process workflows for AM parts — machining strategies, surface treatments, and thermal cycles needed to meet drawings.

roles.title: AM Test Engineer
roles.description: Designs and runs test programs to validate AM part performance — tensile, fatigue, NDT, and acceptance criteria.

roles.title: AM Certification Engineer
roles.description: Drives part-level certification for AM components — assembles qualification dossiers, interfaces with regulators, and owns audit response.

roles.title: Lead AM Post-Processing Engineer
roles.description: Leads post-processing engineering — sets standards across all material/machine combinations and mentors process engineers.

roles.title: AM Standards Engineer
roles.description: Owns standards engineering within the AM team — represents the company at ASTM F42 / ISO TC 261 and internalizes standards into specs.

roles.title: Principal AM Reliability Engineer
roles.description: Principal-level reliability authority on AM parts — owns fatigue testing strategy, failure investigations, and reliability acceptance.

roles.title: Director of AM Quality
roles.description: Owns AM quality function at director level — strategy, regulatory relations, audit response, and quality team org design.

roles.title: AM Logistics Coordinator
roles.description: Coordinates inbound feedstock and outbound finished AM part shipments — manages logistics paperwork and warehouse handoffs.

roles.title: AM Inventory Specialist
roles.description: Tracks AM feedstock inventory and powder lots — cycle counting, lot traceability, and reorder triggers.

roles.title: AM Sales Coordinator
roles.description: Supports AM sales reps — prepares quotes, manages CRM data, and follows up on customer orders.

roles.title: AM Customer Success Specialist
roles.description: Supports AM customers post-sale — application troubleshooting, training escalation, and account health.

roles.title: AM Procurement Analyst
roles.description: Analyzes AM supplier spend and helps qualify new powder/equipment vendors against price and lead-time requirements.

roles.title: AM Account Manager
roles.description: Owns key AM accounts — quota carrier, expansion planning, and primary relationship for assigned customers.

roles.title: AM Marketing Manager
roles.description: Owns AM marketing programs — segment positioning, demand gen, customer events, and technical content.

roles.title: VP of AM Business Development
roles.description: Senior executive over AM business development — strategic partnerships, large account ownership, and revenue scaling.

roles.title: Chief AM Strategy Officer
roles.description: C-suite leader owning AM strategy across the company — market entry, M&A, partnerships, and board reporting.
